engineIntelligence.h2: Fiat Linea Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Fiat Linea in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: Fiat Linea 199A2.000 engine - diesel 1.3L replacement UK
knownFailures: EGR valve clogging - causes loss of power and rough idle from 60k miles on high-mileage units.
Turbo wastegate actuator failure - results in boost pressure loss and reduced performance.
Timing belt wear - interference engine design means belt failure causes catastrophic valve damage.

cards.altText: Fiat Linea 198A3.000 engine - diesel 1.6L replacement UK
knownFailures: Turbocharger actuator fault - common on higher-mileage units causing boost irregularities.
DPF regeneration issues - blocked filters on short-journey vehicles lead to warning lights.
Injector seal leaks - diesel smell and misfires from degraded injector collars.

cards.altText: Fiat Linea 198A4.000 engine - petrol 1.4L replacement UK
knownFailures: Turbo boost leak - intercooler pipe cracks cause reduced power on performance variants.
Timing belt tensioner wear - requires preventative replacement to avoid interference damage.
Oil consumption increase - higher-mileage units may require piston ring attention.

commonProblems.h2: Fiat Linea Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Fiat Linea's 1.3L and 1.6L MultiJet diesel engines dominate UK replacement enquiries, with EGR clogging and turbo actuator faults most common. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address original failure points - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.group: Fiat Linea 1.3 MultiJet EGR Valve Clogging
problems.h4: Fiat Linea 1.3 MultiJet EGR Valve Clogging - Why Power Loss Occurs
problems.affectedModels: Fiat Linea Active, Dynamic, Emotion 1.3 MultiJet (2007-2018, 199A2.000 & A13DTE)
problems.rootCause: Exhaust gas recirculation valve accumulates carbon deposits from diesel combustion, restricting flow and triggering limp mode.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove, clean or replace EGR valve; reset ECU adaptation
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix if carbon buildup mild; replacement recommended above 80k miles

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace EGR valve, cooler, and associated gaskets; full system clean
repairOptions.longevity: More comprehensive; suitable if cooler also clogged; extends service life

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 1.3 MultiJet with upgraded EGR components, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value if engine has other wear; addresses root cause plus prevents future failures

problems.recommendation: On a Fiat Linea worth £800-£2,500, if the engine is otherwise healthy and below 80k miles, an EGR valve replacement offers good value. If the engine has high mileage or multiple issues, a rebuilt unit with upgraded EGR components provides longer-term peace of mind.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 199A2.000 engine prices for your Fiat Linea

problems.h4: Fiat Linea Turbocharger Wastegate Failure - Boost Pressure Loss
problems.rootCause: Turbo wastegate actuator linkage wears or sticks, preventing proper boost control and triggering fault codes.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace wastegate actuator only; calibrate boost pressure
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if turbo core is healthy; quick fix for boost irregularities

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Strip, clean, replace bearings/seals, recalibrate wastegate
repairOptions.longevity: Cost-effective if housing intact; extends turbo life significantly

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt MultiJet with new/reconditioned turbo, upgraded components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if engine has other wear or turbo damage is severe

problems.recommendation: If the turbo housing is undamaged and the engine is otherwise sound, actuator replacement or turbo rebuild offers good value. On high-mileage Lineas with multiple concerns, a full rebuilt engine with upgraded turbo components provides better long-term reliability.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Fiat Linea MultiJet turbo replacement

problems.h4: Fiat Linea Timing Belt Failure - Interference Engine Risk
problems.affectedModels: All Fiat Linea variants (2007-2018, all engine codes)
problems.rootCause: Timing belt stretches or snaps due to age, mileage, or missed service intervals; interference design means valve damage follows.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing belt, tensioner, idlers, water pump; reset service indicator
repairOptions.longevity: Essential preventative maintenance; prevents catastrophic failure if done on schedule

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine after belt failure damage; upgraded components, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Only option if belt snapped and valves damaged; rebuild addresses root cause

problems.recommendation: Never skip timing belt service on the Linea's interference engines. If the belt is intact and mileage is under 60k, preventative replacement is excellent value. If the belt has snapped, a rebuilt engine is the only viable path to get your Linea back on the road reliably.
problems.cta: Compare Fiat Linea timing belt service and engine replacement prices

problems.group: Fiat Linea Fuel Injector Seal Leak
problems.h4: Fiat Linea Fuel Injector Seal Leak - Diesel Smell & Misfires
problems.affectedModels: Fiat Linea 1.3/1.6 MultiJet (2007-2018, all diesel codes)
problems.rootCause: Injector collar seals degrade from heat and fuel exposure, allowing diesel to leak into cylinder head causing smell and misfires.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove injectors, replace copper seals/collars, recode injectors to ECU
repairOptions.longevity: Effective for seal-only leaks; requires specialist coding equipment

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove, test, refurbish or replace injectors; recode and calibrate
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if injectors show wear beyond seals; restores fuel trim accuracy

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt MultiJet with new/reconditioned injectors, upgraded seals, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value if engine has other wear or injector damage is severe

problems.recommendation: For isolated seal leaks on otherwise healthy engines, seal replacement with proper coding is cost-effective. If injectors are worn or the engine has high mileage, a rebuilt unit with upgraded fuel system components offers better long-term value.
problems.cta: Find Fiat Linea injector seal repair and engine replacement quotes

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Fiat Linea
finalCta.paragraph: The Fiat Linea's MultiJet engines are robust when maintained, but EGR, turbo and timing belt issues can escalate quickly. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Fiat Linea specialists who supply rebuilt engines with upgraded components addressing these common failures. Every unit includes a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery options. Compare prices today and keep your Linea on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Fiat Linea Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Fiat Linea and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Fiat Linea Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Fiat Linea is listed below with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the right replacement.

items.title: Fiat Linea 1.3L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.3L MultiJet diesel powered the majority of UK Fiat Lineas from 2007-2018, offering strong fuel economy for high-mileage owners. This compact turbo diesel appeared across Active, Dynamic and Emotion trims, with Euro 4 (199A2.000) and Euro 5 (A13DTE) variants requiring careful code matching for replacement compatibility.
commonFailurePoints: timing belt maintenance oversights on interference design
items.cta: Get quotes for Fiat Linea 1.3L diesel engines

items.title: Fiat Linea 1.6L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.6L MultiJet diesel offered higher output for Emotion-spec Lineas, providing stronger motorway performance while retaining MultiJet efficiency. Available from 2008-2018 in Euro 4 (198A3.000) and Euro 5 (A16DTE) forms, this engine requires precise code verification due to emissions hardware differences affecting replacement compatibility.
commonFailurePoints: DPF regeneration issues on short-journey use
items.cta: Get quotes for Fiat Linea 1.6L diesel engines

items.title: Fiat Linea 1.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The naturally aspirated 1.4L FIRE petrol engine served as the entry-level petrol option for early Linea models, offering simple, affordable motoring with lower replacement costs. The performance-focused 1.4L T-Jet turbo petrol (198A4.000) powered enthusiast Emotion variants, requiring specialist rebuilds to retain factory turbo mapping and boost characteristics.
commonFailurePoints: Timing belt tensioner wear on interference design
turbo boost leaks on T-Jet variants
items.cta: Compare Fiat Linea 1.4L petrol engine prices

families: 199A2.000 - Base Euro 4 1.3L MultiJet (Active/Dynamic), EGR sensitivity common
A13DTE - Revised Euro 5 1.3L MultiJet with DPF, later Dynamic/Emotion models
198A3.000 - Higher-output 1.6L MultiJet for Emotion-spec, turbo actuator wear noted
A16DTE - Euro 5 1.6L MultiJet with updated emissions hardware, final-production models
knownFor: Excellent high-mileage fuel economy on motorway-focused use
EGR valve clogging causing power loss from 60k miles on urban-driven examples
Timing belt critical on interference design - preventative replacement essential
Strong UK replacement demand on Active/Dynamic 1.3L variants due to fleet use

items.title: Fiat Linea Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol Lineas featured the simple 1.4L FIRE engine for budget buyers and the turbocharged 1.4L T-Jet for enthusiasts seeking performance, with distinct replacement considerations for each.

198A4.000 - Performance 1.4L T-Jet turbo, specialist rebuilds retaining factory mapping

engineYears.h2: Fiat Linea Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Fiat Linea's 2007-2018 UK production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The Linea launched with Euro 4 MultiJet diesel and FIRE petrol engines, establishing the 1.3L diesel as the volume seller for UK grey-import buyers.
keyChanges: Fiat Linea introduced to UK market as grey-import saloon option
1.3L MultiJet (199A2.000) and 1.4L FIRE/T-Jet petrol engines available
Manual transmission standard; Dualogic automated manual optional on select trims
knownFor: Strong fuel economy appeal for high-mileage grey-import buyers
Early EGR sensitivity reports on urban-driven 1.3L MultiJet units
T-Jet enthusiast following for turbo petrol performance in saloon form
years.cta: Get quotes for 2007-2009 Fiat Linea replacement engines
years.ticker: Fiat Linea 1.3 MultiJet engine replacement • Fiat Linea T-Jet turbo rebuild • Fiat Linea 2007 engine code match

years.description: Euro 5 emissions compliance introduced revised engine codes with DPF hardware, affecting replacement compatibility for later Linea models.
keyChanges: Euro 5 emissions compliance phased in across diesel range
Revised engine codes: A13DTE (1.3L) and A16DTE (1.6L) with DPF
Emotion 1.6 MultiJet gained popularity for stronger motorway performance
mainEngines: A13DTE - 1.3L diesel Euro 5 with DPF (Dynamic / Emotion)
knownFor: DPF regeneration issues on short-journey urban use requiring active management
Turbo actuator calibration drift reported on higher-mileage 1.6L units
T-Jet retained enthusiast appeal with stable turbo petrol specification
years.cta: Compare Fiat Linea 2010-2013 engine replacement prices
years.ticker: Fiat Linea A13DTE DPF compatibility • Fiat Linea 1.6 MultiJet turbo actuator • Fiat Linea Euro 5 engine swap

years.description: Final-production Lineas emphasised value and reliability refinements, with low-mileage used engines becoming the dominant replacement choice for budget-conscious owners.
keyChanges: Production volumes reduced as Fiat focused on newer platforms
Emphasis on verified low-mileage donor engines for cost-effective replacements
Grey-import compatibility verification tools gained importance for UK buyers
mainEngines: A13DTE - 1.3L diesel Euro 5 (Dynamic / Emotion, final runs)
knownFor: Strong residual value focus with budget rebuilt/used engine demand
Timing belt preventative replacement critical on all interference engines
Grey-import verification essential for correct engine code matching
years.cta: Find Fiat Linea 2014-2018 engine replacement deals
years.ticker: Fiat Linea 2014-2018 low mileage engine • Fiat Linea grey import compatibility • Fiat Linea final production engine code
enquiries: Fiat Linea final production engine code

faq.h2: Fiat Linea Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Fiat Linea engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Typical rebuilt Fiat Linea engines cost £1,400-£1,900 for 1.3L MultiJet units, with used options from £600-£1,200 and premium T-Jet rebuilds reaching £2,200. Prices vary by engine code, mileage and supplier location.
keyPoints: 1.3L MultiJet rebuilt: £1,400-£1,900 supply only
Used verified low-mileage: £600-£1,200 depending on donor history
1.4L T-Jet performance rebuild: £1,800-£2,200 due to specialist requirements
Supply & fit adds £400-£800 depending on garage location and ancillary parts
items.cta: Compare Fiat Linea engine replacement prices from UK specialists

items.question: What engine code does my Fiat Linea have and where do I find it?
items.answer: Fiat Linea engine codes appear on the engine block stamping, V5C logbook, or via DVLA reg check. Common codes include 199A2.000 (1.3L diesel), A13DTE (Euro 5 1.3L), and 198A4.000 (1.4L T-Jet).
keyPoints: Check V5C registration document section D.2 for engine code reference
Engine block stamping visible near gearbox housing on MultiJet units
DVLA reg lookup instantly confirms code, fuel type and variant compatibility
Grey-import Lineas require European spec verification for correct code match
items.cta: Verify your Fiat Linea engine code with our free reg checker

items.question: Is the Fiat Linea 1.3 MultiJet engine reliable long-term?
items.answer: The 1.3L MultiJet is robust when maintained, but EGR clogging and turbo actuator wear are common above 60k miles. Regular servicing and timely belt replacement prevent most major failures.
keyPoints: EGR valve cleaning every 40k miles reduces power loss risk on urban use
Timing belt replacement at 60k miles critical on interference engine design
Turbo actuator calibration checks recommended during major services
Overall reliability good for high-mileage motorway-focused Linea owners
items.cta: Get quotes for reliable rebuilt Fiat Linea 1.3 MultiJet engines

items.question: Can I fit a Fiat Linea engine myself or do I need a specialist?
items.answer: While technically possible for experienced DIYers, Fiat Linea engine replacement requires specialist tools for ECU coding, timing alignment and emissions system calibration - professional fitting is strongly recommended.
keyPoints: ECU immobiliser coding requires dealer-level diagnostic equipment
MultiJet timing belt alignment needs precision tools to avoid valve damage
DPF and EGR systems require post-installation adaptation resets
Supply & fit service includes warranty coverage DIY installs cannot offer
items.cta: Book Fiat Linea engine supply & fit with vetted UK specialists

items.question: What warranty comes with a Fiat Linea replacement engine?
items.answer: Engines sourced through EngineMarket include a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from vetted UK specialists, covering parts and labour for manufacturing defects on rebuilt units.
keyPoints: 12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard on rebuilt/remanufactured units
Used engines typically carry 3-6 month warranty depending on supplier
Warranty covers internal components; ancillaries excluded unless specified
Always confirm exact terms with chosen supplier before commitment
items.cta: Compare Fiat Linea engine warranty terms across UK suppliers

items.question: How long does Fiat Linea engine replacement take from order to completion?
items.answer: Supply-only engines typically dispatch within 1-3 working days; supply & fit appointments require 3-7 days for engine sourcing, delivery and garage scheduling depending on location and variant.
keyPoints: In-stock rebuilt engines: 1-2 day dispatch to UK mainland
Made-to-order rebuilds: 5-10 working days build time plus delivery
Supply & fit: 3-7 days total including garage booking and installation
Next-day delivery available on select in-stock units for urgent replacements
items.cta: Check Fiat Linea engine availability and delivery timelines

items.question: Will a replacement Fiat Linea engine pass MoT emissions testing?
items.answer: Yes - rebuilt engines supplied through EngineMarket include MoT emissions compliance certification, with DPF/EGR systems calibrated to pass UK testing standards for your Linea's registration year.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engines bench-tested to meet original emissions specifications
DPF-equipped codes (A13DTE/A16DTE) include regeneration system verification
EGR calibration reset ensures NOx levels comply with MoT limits
Certificate of compliance provided with every rebuilt engine dispatch
items.cta: Get MoT-compliant Fiat Linea engine replacement quotes

items.question: Is a Fiat Linea diesel engine ULEZ compliant in London?
items.answer: Most Fiat Linea diesels are Euro 4 or Euro 5, meaning pre-2015 registrations generally do not meet London ULEZ Euro 6 diesel standards - check your exact registration date and engine code for compliance status.
keyPoints: Euro 4 diesel Lineas (2007-2010) do not meet ULEZ Euro 6 requirement
Euro 5 diesel Lineas (2011-2015) still fall short of ULEZ Euro 6 standard
Petrol Lineas must meet Euro 4 minimum; most 2007+ models comply
Engine replacement does not change vehicle's ULEZ classification based on registration date
items.cta: Check your Fiat Linea ULEZ status before engine replacement

items.question: Can I upgrade my Fiat Linea from 1.3L to 1.6L MultiJet engine?
items.answer: Physically possible but not recommended - ECU, wiring loom, transmission calibration and emissions system differences make 1.3L to 1.6L swaps complex, costly and potentially non-compliant with UK regulations.
keyPoints: ECU and immobiliser systems are code-specific and not cross-compatible
Transmission gear ratios and clutch specifications differ between codes
Emissions certification would require re-registration as modified vehicle
Cost of conversion typically exceeds value of higher-output rebuilt 1.3L unit
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Fiat Linea 1.3L vs 1.6L MultiJet engine prices

items.question: What is the difference between rebuilt, remanufactured and used Fiat Linea engines?
items.answer: Used engines are low-mileage donor units with minimal reconditioning; rebuilt engines receive comprehensive strip/replace with upgrades for known failures; remanufactured units meet factory specs using OEM components throughout.
keyPoints: Used: Fastest, cheapest option (£600-£1,200) with 3-6 month warranty
Rebuilt: Best value balance (£1,400-£1,900) with failure-point upgrades, 12-month warranty
Remanufactured: Premium option (£1,800-£2,200) with OEM-spec components for T-Jet preservation
All options available supply-only or supply & fit through EngineMarket network
items.cta: Compare Fiat Linea engine types and find the best value for your budget
